mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat from a restrictive level, equity discount rates ease faster than earnings expectations deteriorate, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with non-increasing FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation deceleration after prior restraint lowers expected terminal rates and real yields, supporting a positive S&P 500 response over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation decelerations are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening income growth and higher recession risk, which reduces expected corporate sales and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in most unemployment-spike episodes, or if the signal has no incremental explanatory power after controlling for recent price trend.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening household income and retail demand, lifting near-term revenue expectations and pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvements do not predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains all observed effect.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO after a prior slump indicates industrial supply normalization and better corporate output, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO recoveries are followed by negative or zero 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal vanishes outside recession rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead cyclical demand and household confidence; when HOUST breaks higher from a depressed level, the S&P 500 tends to rise over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 5% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if year-over-year HOUST growth fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is reversed during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and labor creation accelerate together, broad nominal demand is strengthening and equity earnings expectations improve, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and PAYEMS both rise above their trailing 4-quarter and 3-month averages, respectively
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and PAYEMS strength does not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the joint signal is negative in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary regime, high FEDFUNDS suppresses valuation multiples and amplifies bad-news sensitivity from inflation and activity data, so S&P 500 returns over the next week are negative when policy remains tight.
failure_condition: Reject if tight-policy windows do not show below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
